sibling_review.summary: Docs-only PR marking Phase 2 complete. PLAN.md markers, ADR-026 acceptance, journal CLI, legacy migration, and .memoryAccess audit category all verified against code and git history; swift test shows no regression (exit=1 MLX metallib abort is pre-existing on main a08c345; 135 tests executed, 0 failures on both). However the implementation summary overclaims the delivered PBAC surface in four places, and the three new routing attestations lack the envelope_hash/signature integrity fields carried by existing attestations.

findings.issue: Claims `ProfileService.routableContext(filter:)` filters values/goals/boundaries by purpose intersection and deny set. The implemented signature is `routableContext(purposes:)` (ProfileService.swift:146) and it performs NO filtering — it returns RoutableProfileContext(profile:) in full, with the code comment stating purposes is only a choke point for future filtering.
findings.remediation: Correct the claim to match the implementation: rename to routableContext(purposes:) and state that per-purpose filtering of values/goals/boundaries is not yet implemented, or implement the filtering.

findings.issue: Claims `deniedPurposes` support on `TemporalFact` and `EpisodicGist`, and AccessPurpose/AccessScope labelling on values, goals, boundaries, temporal facts, and episodic gists. Only `TemporalFact` (Models.swift:351-352) and `JournalEntry` (Models.swift:226) carry accessScope; only TemporalFact has deniedPurposes. `EpisodicGist` (Models.swift:404) has neither field, and Value/Goal/Boundary have no purpose labelling. ADR-026 proposed deniedPurposes on both TemporalFact and EpisodicGist, so the Accepted status plus 'implemented and delivered in PR #29' overstates delivery.
findings.remediation: Restrict the claims to TemporalFact (and JournalEntry accessScope), and either implement EpisodicGist purpose labelling or record it as a deliberate deviation in ADR-026 before marking it Accepted.

findings.issue: Claims every PBAC access decision (allow and deny) is logged under .memoryAccess. No deny decision is logged anywhere in Council/Sources; only aggregate 'allowed' entries are written (MemoryService.facts(subject:purposes:), DeliberationService). Store-level denials in GRDBMemoryStore are silent filter exclusions.
findings.remediation: Reword to 'allowed decisions are logged' or add deny-decision audit entries.

findings.issue: The three new attestations are well-formed JSON with required scalar fields and a valid expiry (2026-07-22), but omit `envelope_hash` and `signature`, which both pre-existing attestations (23.json, 26.json) carry, and omit `base_sha` inside runtime_binding. With agent_integration.attested=true and kid 'legacy-hmac', these envelopes cannot be integrity-verified. Additionally, runtime_binding.branch in 16.json (sdl/issue-16) and 25.json (sdl/issue-25-sqlcipher) does not match the branch they are committed on (sdl/issue-23-phase-2-completion).
findings.remediation: Regenerate the attestations through the signing path so they carry envelope_hash/signature and base_sha, and either commit 16.json/25.json on their own branches or document why they are staged here.

findings.issue: Commit trailers verified: SDL-Commit-Author, SDL-Routing-Attestation: 23, Task-Ref: issue-23-completion, and Lifecycle-Record-ID e0e48a68 matches the node_id/lifecycle_record_id in 23-completion.json.

Phase 2 markers verified accurate: profile ingestion merged as PR #28 and PBAC as PR #29 (both confirmed in main git history); SQLCipher issue #25 confirmed OPEN on GitHub, consistent with the in-progress marker. Journal CLI (add/list/remove with --tag/--date/--stdin/--reveal/--limit), legacy journalExcerpts migration, and userInspection-only journal scoping all verified in code.

swift test on the PR branch executes 135 tests with 0 failures but exits 1 due to an MLX 'Failed to load the default metallib' abort at the end of the run. Reproduced identically on main (a08c345), so it is a pre-existing environment limitation (already documented in the summary), not a regression from this docs-only PR.
findings.remediation: None for this PR; consider gating the MLX-loading test path so the suite exits 0 off-device.
